plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs, aligning with capital preservation and removing below-stop holdings.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure for stability.
Buy a 5% position in NOW, a high-quality name with a strong scanner read.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by reducing leverage and concentration while adding high-quality, stable assets.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% monthly returns with a focus on capital preservation and minimal drawdown. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering overall portfolio concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name mandate and reduce overall concentration.
Sell SOXL to remove a below-stop holding and reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce overall leverage while maintaining a small speculative position.
Increase QQQI to 22% to maintain a balanced income component.
Buy 5% ANET and 3% TECL to add quality tech and diversified exposure from strong scanner setups.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer and reducing overall leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% monthly returns, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration from your current book while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate volatility and a more balanced risk-reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to exit the below-stop holding and free capital for stronger setups.
Add to SPAL, increasing leveraged ETF exposure to 5% within mandate limits.
Increase CRWD to 18% to press a current winning position.
Buy 7% TECL and 7% ARM, focusing on high-scoring leveraged tech and chip exposure from the scanner.
This plan aggressively leans into strong scanner setups and uses leverage for amplified growth potential.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% monthly returns, aiming for stretched growth. It involves higher leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups, which can lead to significantly larger drawdowns and rapid losses. Be aware of daily decay in leveraged ETFs and strict adherence to stops is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Increase QQQI to 35% to heavily favor income and covered-call ETFs.
Trim QQQ to 10%, SMH to 5%, and CRWD to 5% to reduce growth-oriented and high-beta exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs, aligning with the low-drawdown income strategy.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown by rotating into income-focused ETFs and reducing high-beta holdings.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% monthly distributions, focusing on parking cash for yield. It significantly reduces growth potential and high-beta exposure for stability. Remember, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is not a savings account.
